seniority.level: Senior IC (likely Senior TPM / Staff TPM)
level_signals: Several years of experience in technical program management required
Comp range $290K-$435K base, consistent with senior to staff-level IC at Anthropic
Expected to act as primary technical interface to major cloud partner engineering orgs
Drives executive-level reporting and cross-organizational alignment

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have several years of experience in technical program management, with a track record of successfully delivering complex technical programs, preferably involving cloud platforms and AI technologies.
Have strong understanding of cloud computing architectures, AI/ML deployment, and integration challenges.
Have exceptional interpersonal and communication skills, enabling you to influence without authority and build cross-organizational support.
Have a high threshold for navigating ambiguity and ability to balance strategic priorities with rapid, high-quality execution.
Thrive in fast-paced, scaling environments with the ability to bring order to chaos.
Are passionate about Anthropic's mission and committed to ensuring AI is developed safely.
Minimum education: Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Direct experience with a hyperscaler's managed AI platform — Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, or Azure AI Foundry — including how partners list, launch, and onboard customers on it.
Background in ML inference, model serving infrastructure, or accelerator-based compute.
Have owned joint engineering roadmap or dependency tracking, driving incident follow-through, and converting open issues into a prioritized plan both sides commit to
Experience with release engineering, deployment automation, or CI/CD for systems that ship to multiple targets or environments.

scope.surface: Cloud inference deployments of Claude models on Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex, and Microsoft Foundry
scope.business_model: B2B cloud AI partnerships (third-party platform distribution of AI models)
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time delivery of cloud model launches
Cross-partner roadmap alignment and execution velocity
core_responsibilities: Partner with engineering leaders to define, scope, and sequence major technical initiatives for cloud partnerships and AI model deployment
Own launch readiness for Claude models on partner cloud platforms: checklist, blocker tracking, joint go/no-go with the partner, and post-launch stability follow-through
Act as the primary technical interface to cloud partner engineering orgs — owning the relationship, the shared roadmap, and day-to-day coordination on deployment, capacity, and incidents
Drive cross-functional alignment across internal engineering, product, and go-to-market teams to land joint deliverables with the partner
Provide clear and transparent reporting on program status, issues, and risks to executives and stakeholders

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and wide ($290K-$435K base), reflecting senior to staff-level banding
Clear ownership scope: primary technical interface to major hyperscaler partners
Concrete responsibilities defined around launch readiness, partner roadmap, and incident management
Encourages applications even from candidates who don't meet every qualification
Explicit acknowledgment of underrepresented groups and imposter syndrome
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Compensation range is unusually wide ($145K spread), suggesting significant level uncertainty or strong internal leveling discretion
Years of experience requirement stated only as 'several years' with no numeric floor — ambiguous for screening purposes
Primary cloud partner named in posting title is 'Cloud Inference' but body names Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex, and Microsoft Foundry — no single cloud focus, requiring breadth across all three

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and scope of responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
seniority.years_required_min (stated only as 'several years'; no numeric minimum given)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not in JD)
location.cities_listed (San Francisco inferred from HQ mention; no specific city named for this role in body)
ambiguities: Years of experience: JD says 'several years' and separately 'Minimum years of experience: Years of experience required will correlate with the internal job level requirements for the position' — no numeric minimum can be determined; left as null.

The role is titled 'Cloud Inference' but the body covers three distinct hyperscaler platforms (Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex, Microsoft Foundry); it is unclear whether the TPM owns all three or a subset.
Equity: JD mentions 'optional equity donation matching' in company benefits copy, implying equity exists in the package but does not explicitly confirm equity is part of this role's offer.
